successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveRequest, CreateLeaveRequestDto, LeavePolicy, LeaveBalance, Employee, and AuditLog interfaces
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports ILeaveRepository interface and LeaveRepository class implementing it
src/modules/leave/policy.repository.ts exists and exports ILeavePolicyRepository interface and LeavePolicyRepository class implementing it
src/modules/leave/balance.repository.ts exists and exports ILeaveBalanceRepository interface and LeaveBalanceRepository class implementing it
All repository interfaces have concrete implementations (even if methods throw 'Not implemented')
All TypeScript interfaces match the canonical SQL schema column definitions exactly
ARCHITECTURE.md is updated to include the new leave module structure with repository interfaces

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces
All TypeScript interfaces must match canonical SQL schema column definitions exactly
Repository methods must throw 'Not implemented' as placeholders for phase 2
Module structure must follow modular-monolith architecture with dependency direction: leave -> employee, leave -> policy, leave -> balance
All repository classes must implement their respective interfaces
outOfScope: Database implementation of repository methods (deferred to phase 2)
Leave balance domain models and repository in src/modules/balance/ (deferred to phase 2)
Employee domain models and repository in src/modules/employee/ (deferred to phase 3)
Leave policy domain models and repository in src/modules/policy/ (deferred to phase 4)
Leave service interface and implementation (deferred to phase 5)
Comprehensive error handling and logging (deferred to phase 9)
Leave controller and API routes (deferred to phase 10)
Actual database queries or ORM integration

ambiguities.description: The architecture-agent provided interfaces for Employee and AuditLog in leave.model.ts, but these domains belong to other modules (employee and shared/audit). Should these interfaces be included in leave.model.ts or imported from their respective modules?
The intent mentions 'Jest unit tests' but doesn't specify test coverage requirements. Should tests verify interface contracts only, or also test repository method stubs?
options: Test repository class structure with stubs
Test both interfaces and repository structure
